reasoning:
The reference class here is the occurrence of 'Battles' (using ACLED's definition) in Kuwait. The base rate for this is 0 - there are virtually no internal armed interactions of this sort in Kuwait in recent history, and ACLED indicates a 360-day average of exactly 0.000 leading up to 2025-12-11. I searched extensively whether there have been any attacks, drone intercepts, or shootouts with militants in late 2025, and found nothing. I've also verified through the ACLED conflict guidelines that technicalities (like interception of a drone or a unilateral remote blast) generally don’t qualify as a ‘Battle’ anyway. A ‘Battle’ typically requires some form of violent interaction between two organized armed groups (like regular army and a militia, or two militias) on the ground. 

Since the base rate is 0, the target number for the 30-day period before any of the resolution dates is technically any positive value greater than the 0.000 average (meaning at least 1 battle). Given Kuwait's internal peace and stability since the Gulf War, this is highly unlikely to happen. The only risks are severe regional escalation that ends up involving a ground clash or an incredibly unlikely coordinated homegrown insurgency/terror incident meeting the 'Battle' criteria (most terror attacks are 'Violence against civilians' or 'Explosions/Remote violence').

The probability for the first date (2025-12-28) is set at 2% because most of the 30 days have already elapsed without a battle happening. The probability is progressively higher for the future dates (up to 5% for March 2026) due to increasing uncertainty.
